Aug 15, 2016 CalNet IDs CONTENTS What are CalNet IDs? UC Extension & Concurrent Enrollment students Other non-UCB visitors Restrictions on non-UCB students How New Instructors Get CalNet IDs CalNet IDs and Cardkeys What

ors Get CalNet IDs CalNet IDs and Cardkeys What are CalNet IDs? -------------------- A CalNet ID is a combination of your Student ID (or Employee ID) number and a password. A CalNet ID is used to logon to many services on campus that auth

d to logon to many services on campus that authenticate using WEB sites. These include: - AirBears2 wireless network (http://airbears.berkeley.edu/) - campus software downloads (http://software.berkeley.edu/) - CalMail email service (http:

re.berkeley.edu/) - CalMail email service (http://calmail.berkeley.edu/) UCB students are issued their initial CalNet username (their SID number) and a password via their orientation information. UCB staff are issued their initial CalNe

on. UCB staff are issued their initial CalNet username (their EID number) after they have been entered in to HR database, and they go to a CalNet deputy for the initial password. Please see https://calnet.berkeley.edu/ for more infor

se see https://calnet.berkeley.edu/ for more information. UC Extension & Concurrent Enrollment students --------------------------------------------- UC Berkeley Extension Concurrent Enrollment students, please see http://extension.berkeley.edu

udents, please see http://extension.berkeley.edu/static/studentservices/concurrent/#enroll You have to get approval for your courses first, then the UC Extension office will issue you an Extension ID and PIN that you use to obtain a CalNet I

ion ID and PIN that you use to obtain a CalNet ID. These will provide limited access to UCB services. Access to the campus software downloads are excluded. UC Berkeley Extension students are NOT issued UC Berkeley CAL1 cards (aka "studen

NOT issued UC Berkeley CAL1 cards (aka "student id cards", "cardkeys"). Some departments can issue temporary cardkeys for visitors such as UC Berkeley Extension students, for a fee. (For more information: http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/c

ore information: http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/cgi-bin/pub.cgi?file=cardkey.help#5) Beginning August 15 2016, UC Extension students can login to CalCentral (http://calcentral.berkeley.edu) to apply for concurrent enrollment in classes.

apply for concurrent enrollment in classes. Other non-UCB visitors ---------------------- There are 2 types of CalNet IDs for visitors: CalNet Guest Accounts: typically for people who are not paid by UCB CalNet Affiliate Accounts: typica

ot paid by UCB CalNet Affiliate Accounts: typically for people who are paid by UCB These will provide limited access to UCB services. Access to the campus wireless (AirBears2) and VPN networks are excluded. For information, please see

s are excluded. For information, please see https://wikihub.berkeley.edu/display/calnet/CalNet+Guest+and+Affiliate+Accounts http://kb.berkeley.edu/kb207 http://kb.berkeley.edu/kb517 http://kb.berkeley.edu/kb3027 Restrictions on non-UC

://kb.berkeley.edu/kb3027 Restrictions on non-UCB students -------------------------------- If you are enrolled in a UCB class through UC Extension, Concurrent Enrollment or etc, you may not be allowed to download free UCB-licensed software

allowed to download free UCB-licensed software from http://software.berkeley.edu or http://cab.berkeley.edu. The licensing for Exceed is controlled by the UC Berkeley Office of Software Distribution. Only students who have valid UCB student

ibution. Only students who have valid UCB student IDs can buy or download Exceed for free from UCB. For alternatives, please see http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/cgi-bin/pub.cgi?file=exceed.help. CalView students, please see http://inst.ee

CalView students, please see http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/cgi-bin/pub.cgi?file=calview.help. How New Instructors Get CalNet IDs ---------------------------------- 1) get employee ID (EID) number via HR 2) get Cal1 card from Cal Photo I

number via HR 2) get Cal1 card from Cal Photo ID office (http://calphotoid.berkeley.edu/) 3) get "token" from calNet deputy 4) set Calnet friendly name and password at https://calnet.berkeley.edu It takes at least 24 hours for other servi

du It takes at least 24 hours for other services on campus to sync up with the new HR data. Tese are some of the services that become available: * carkey access (using the prox number from the back of the ID card) * AirBears2 campus w

om the back of the ID card) * AirBears2 campus wireless service; see http://airbears.berkeley.edu * course enrollments, etc on http://bearfacts.berkeley.edu * course WEB services on http://bspace.berkeley.edu * ___@berkeley.edu email account;

e.berkeley.edu * ___@berkeley.edu email account; go to http://calmail.berkeley.edu * Personnel info at https://blu.berkeley.edu CalNet IDs and Cardkeys ----------------------- With a CalNet ID (https://calnet.berkeley.edu/), you can get a CAL

(https://calnet.berkeley.edu/), you can get a CAL1 card (https://services.housing.berkeley.edu/c1c/static/index.htm). CAL1 cards are also referred to as "student id cards", "SID cards" and "cardkeys". The CAL1 card will have a cardkey num
dkeys". The CAL1 card will have a cardkey number on the back lower right corner. Department staff can use that number to give you cardkey access to their buildings. EECS Instructional Support 37
nal Support 378/384/386 Cory, 333 Soda inst@eecs.berkeley.edu
